assesment:
Rosacea; not at treatment goal
 Management options were discussed, including: systemic therapy, including oral antibiotics, sulfa wash, and topical therapy. 
Risks and benefits of all options were reviewed.
Patient opted to proceed with: 
-Apply ivermectin once daily to the affected areas.- Discussed goals of using Soolantra with patient, including reducing inflammation and redness associated with Rosacea. Patient reports no allergies to Soolantra (ivermectin). Advised patient that Soolantra should only be used on clean, dry skin. Most common side effects including but not limited to: irritation, dryness, itching and mild burning at the application site reviewed with patient. Soolantra should not be applied near the eyes and hands should be washed after application. Advised patient to use sunscreen and protective clothings to avoid sunburn as the medication can make the skin more sensitive to sunlight. 
- cont w IPL
 -Apply a thin layer of erythro/ BPO 5% to face once daily. Advised patient that BPO may bleach hair or fabrics. Use carefully, avoid contact with clothing, hair and surroundings. ClindaGel is a topical gel formulation of the antibiotic Clindamycin. Patient reports no allergies to this. Advised patient to avoid contact with eyes and mouth. Most common side effects include dryness, peeling, itching, or redness at application site. Advised patient to minimize sun exposure and use sunscreen as this medication may increase sensitivity to sunlight.
Follow up: after 1 Month for Follow up with Irene Sawyer , FNP-BC

assesment:
Aesthetics - Wrinkles
 Botox done today. 
Discussed with patient that Botox is a type of neuromodulator that causes temporary paralyzation of relaxation of the muscle to reduce the formation of wrinkles. Patient aware that results take around 2 weeks to take full effect. Results are not permanent and will need re-treatment. Patient aware of R/B/SE/A of neuromodulator treatment. Patient aware that adverse effects can cause headache, bruising, pain, twitching, asymmetry, brow or eyelid ptosis, rash, inability to frown and raise eyebrows, and difficulty swallowing if the neck is treated. 
Aesthetic - Laser
 Laser done today. 
 
IPL first session today 
skin rejuvenation settings used
procedure:
Neuromodulator: Botox
Lot#D0122C4
Expiration date:03/2027
Discussed all common benefits, including improvement in skin lines by paralyzing certain groups of muscles which may lasts from 3-7 months and risks associated with the procedure which includes but is not limited to swelling, rash, headache, local numbness, pain at injection site, bruising, ptosis, drooping of eyelids, and asymmetry of facial lines.Although rare, the patient was informed that infection, respiratory problems and allergic reactions may occur. The patient denies any history of allergies to botulinum toxin or any of its ingredients, denies history of Guillaine Barre syndrome or any neurological problems. Prior to starting, skin was prepared with alcohol. Using a syringe, botulinum toxin was injected to the indicated areas. Patient tolerated the procedure well. Advised patient to avoid laying flat for the next 2 hours.
Neuromodulator (NeuroMod)
